Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting the recognition of certain MÃ©tis governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan, to give effect to treaties with those governments and to make consequential amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act
Identification.RunningHead: Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Indigenous and Northern Affairs as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on FebruaryÂ 8, 2024

Provision.Text: This enactment provides for the recognition of certain MÃ©tis governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and provides a framework for the implementation of treaties entered into by those MÃ©tis governments and the Government of Canada. Finally, it makes consequential amendments to other Acts.

Heading.TitleText: An Act respecting the recognition of certain MÃ©tis governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan, to give effect to treaties with those governments and to make consequential amendments to other Acts

TableOfProvisions.Label: â

Text.XRefExternal: Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Order in council â coming into force
Order in council â coming into force
Judicial notice of MÃ©tis government laws
Amendment or removal of information in schedule

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to achieving reconciliation with the MÃ©tis through renewed government-to-government relationships based on the recognition of rights, respect, cooperation and partnership;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that the MÃ©tis Nation of Alberta, MÃ©tis Nation of Ontario and MÃ©tis Nation â Saskatchewan are authorized to act on behalf of the MÃ©tis collectivities for which they are the democratic representative bodies;
Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the inherent right of self-government of those MÃ©tis collectivities within the framework of the Constitution of Canada;
Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that the inherent right of self-government is a right recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982;

Whereas the MÃ©tis Nation of Alberta, MÃ©tis Nation of Ontario and MÃ©tis Nation â Saskatchewan have signed self-government recognition and implementation agreements with the Government of Canada on February 23 and 24, 2023, and those agreements contemplate the parties negotiating self-government treaties;

Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples and the parties negotiating those treaties view them as contributing to the implementation of that declaration;
And whereas ratification of those treaties requires the coming into force of an Act of Parliament that gives effect to them;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: MÃ©tis governmentâmeans a government that is set out in column 1 of the schedule.â(gouvernement mÃ©tis)
MÃ©tis government lawâmeans a law that is made by a MÃ©tis government and that is referred to in section 9.â(loi du gouvernement mÃ©tis)

supplementary self-government agreementâmeans, except in section 11, a supplementary self-government agreement that is entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada and that is in force, as amended from time to time in accordance with its provisions.â(accord complÃ©mentaire en matiÃ¨re dâautonomie gouvernementale)

tax treatment agreementâmeans a tax treatment agreement that is entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada, as amended from time to time in accordance with its provisions.â(accord sur le traitement fiscal)

treatyâmeans, except in sections 4.1 and 5, a treaty that is entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada that is set out in column 3 of the schedule and that is in force, as amended from time to time in accordance with its provisions.â(traitÃ©)

Section.Text: For greater certainty, each treaty is a treaty within the meaning of sections 25 and 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in this Act is to be construed as abrogating or derogating from the protection provided for the rights of the Indigenous peoples of Canada by the recognition and affirmation of those rights in section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of Indigenous peoples of Canada
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (1), Indigenous peoples of Canada has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: The purposes of this Act are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: advance, through government-to-government relationships, the recognition of the distinct identities, cultures and governance structures of the MÃ©tis;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: advance the recognition of the right to self-determination, including the inherent right of self-government recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act 1982, of certain MÃ©tis collectivities and the recognition of the authority of MÃ©tis governments to act on behalf of those collectivities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide a framework for the implementation of treaties entered into by MÃ©tis governments and His Majesty in right of Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contribute to the implementation of the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples.

Subsection.Text: If a treaty is entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations must cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament a copy of the treaty on any of the first 10 days on which that House is sitting after the treaty is entered into.
After it is tabled, the treaty stands referred to the standing committee of each House of Parliament that normally considers matters relating to Indigenous peoples.
For the purpose of subsection (2), Indigenous peoples has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.MarginalNote: Order in council â coming into force
Section.Text: If the Governor in Council is satisfied that the requirements for the coming into force of a treaty entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada that are set out in the treaty have been met, the Governor in Council may, by order, no earlier than 30 sitting days after the treaty has been tabled under section 4.1,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fix the day on which the treaty comes into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: add the information needed to identify the treaty and the day on which it comes into force to columns 3 and 4, respectively, of the schedule opposite the MÃ©tis government set out in column 1 that is a party to the treaty.

Subsection.Text: A treaty is approved, given effect and declared valid and has the force of law.

For greater certainty, a person or body has the powers, rights, privileges and benefits conferred on the person or body by a treaty and must perform the duties, and is subject to the liabilities, imposed on the person or body by the treaty.
For greater certainty, a treaty is binding on, and may be relied on by, all persons and bodies.

In the event of any inconsistency or conflict between a treaty and the provisions of this Act or any other Act of Parliament, or of any regulation made under this Act or any other Act of Parliament, the treaty prevails to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.

In the event of any inconsistency or conflict between the provisions of this Act and the provisions of any other Act of Parliament, or of any regulation made under any other Act of Parliament, the provisions of this Act prevail to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.

The Government of Canada recognizes that a MÃ©tis government set out in column 1 of the schedule is an Indigenous governing body that is authorized to act on behalf of the MÃ©tis collectivity, including its citizens, set out in column 2 opposite that MÃ©tis government and that the MÃ©tis collectivity holds the right to self-determination, including the inherent right of self-government recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

In subsection (1), Indigenous governing body means a council, government or other entity that is authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group, community or people that holds rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 and, for greater certainty, includes a MÃ©tis government.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in this Act is to be construed as abrogating or derogating from the right to self-determination of a MÃ©tis collectivity that has not authorized a MÃ©tis government set out in column 1 of the schedule to act on its behalf, including the inherent right of self-government recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

A MÃ©tis government that is a party to a treaty has the jurisdiction set out in the treaty, including the authority to make laws in relation to the matters set out in the treaty and the authority to administer and enforce those laws.
A MÃ©tis government is not a federal board, commission or other tribunal, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Courts Act.

Subsection.Text: If a supplementary self-government agreement is entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations must cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament a copy of the agreement on any of the first 10 days on which that House is sitting after the agreement is entered into.
After it is tabled, the agreement stands referred to the standing committee of each House of Parliament that normally considers matters relating to Indigenous peoples.
For the purpose of subsection (2), Indigenous peoples has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.MarginalNote: Order in council â coming into force
Section.Text: If the Governor in Council is satisfied that the requirements for the coming into force of a supplementary self-government agreement entered into by a MÃ©tis government and His Majesty in right of Canada have been met, the Governor in Council may, by order, no earlier than 30 sitting days after the agreement has been tabled under section 10.1, fix the day on which that agreement comes into force.

Subsection.Text: A supplementary self-government agreement is approved, given effect and declared valid and has the force of law.

For greater certainty, a person or body has the powers, rights, privileges and benefits conferred on the person or body by a supplementary self-government agreement and must perform the duties, and is subject to the liabilities, imposed on the person or body by the supplementary self-government agreement.
For greater certainty, a supplementary self-government agreement is binding on, and may be relied on by, all persons and bodies.

Section.Text: A tax treatment agreement is approved, given effect and declared valid and has the force of law during the period that it is in effect.
A tax treatment agreement does not form part of a treaty, and it is not a treaty or a land claims agreement within the meaning of sections 25 and 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Subsection.Text: Judicial notice must be taken of a treaty, supplementary self-government agreement and tax treatment agreement.
Each treaty, supplementary self-government agreement and tax treatment agreement must be published by the Kingâs Printer.

A copy of a treaty, supplementary self-government agreement or tax treatment agreement published by the Kingâs Printer is evidence of the treaty, supplementary self-government agreement or tax treatment agreement and of its contents and a copy purporting to be published by the Kingâs Printer is presumed to be so published unless the contrary is shown.

Section.MarginalNote: Judicial notice of MÃ©tis government laws

Subsection.Text: Judicial notice must be taken of a MÃ©tis government law that is included in a public register of MÃ©tis government laws in accordance with the applicable treaty.
A copy of a MÃ©tis government law purporting to be included in a public register of MÃ©tis government laws is evidence of that law and of its contents unless the contrary is shown.

If an issue arises in any judicial or administrative proceeding in respect of the interpretation, validity or applicability of a provision of this Act, a treaty, a supplementary self-government agreement or a MÃ©tis government law, then the issue must not be decided until the party raising the issue has served notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the Attorney General of Canada and each MÃ©tis government in the case of a provision of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the Attorney General of Canada and the MÃ©tis government that is a party to the treaty in the case of a provision of a treaty;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on the Attorney General of Canada, the MÃ©tis government that is a party to the agreement and the Attorney General of a province, if any, that is a party to the agreement in the case of a provision of a supplementary self-government agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: on the MÃ©tis government that made the law in the case of a provision of a MÃ©tis government law.

Subsection.Text: The notice must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: describe the proceeding;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: state the subject matter of the issue;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: state the date on which the issue is to be argued;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: give the particulars that are necessary to show the point to be argued; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: be served at least 30 days before the day on which the issue is to be argued unless the court or tribunal authorizes a shorter period.

Subsection.Text: In any proceeding, the recipient of a notice may appear and participate as a party with the same rights as any other party.
For greater certainty, subsections (2) and (3) do not require that an oral hearing be held if one is not otherwise required.

Section.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to a MÃ©tis government law.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make any orders and regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary for the purpose of carrying out any of the provisions of this Act, a treaty, a supplementary self-government agreement, a tax treatment agreement or any other agreement related to the implementation of a treaty.

The Governor in Council must ensure that the MÃ©tis government that is a party to the treaty or agreement has a meaningful opportunity to collaborate in the policy development leading to the making of the regulations for the purpose of carrying out a provision of a treaty or agreement.

Section.MarginalNote: Amendment or removal of information in schedule
Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, on the recommendation of the Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations, amend the schedule to amend or remove any information in column 1, 2, 3 or 4 if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the MÃ©tis government set out in that column 1 or set out in column 1 opposite that information has consented to the amendment or removal.

Any action taken or decision made by a MÃ©tis government before the day on which the treaty to which it is a party came into force is deemed to have been validly taken or made to the extent that the action taken or decision made would have been valid under the treaty.

Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (i), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (j) and by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8(2)(f) of the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (vii):

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8(7) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (i), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (j) and by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section.

Section.Text: Subsection 19(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (f), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Yale First Nation Final Agreement Act.
If section 20 of the other Act comes into force before section 22 of this Act, then that section 22 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (j), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section.

Subsection.Text: If section 22 of this Act comes into force before section 20 of the other Act, then that section 20 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (j), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the Yale First Nation Government, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Yale First Nation Final Agreement Act.

Subsection.Text: If section 20 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 22 of this Act, then that section 20 is deemed to have come into force before that section 22 and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.
If section 23 of the other Act comes into force before subsection 23(2) of this Act, then that subsection 23(2) is replaced by the following:
Subsection 8(7) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (j), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: a MÃ©tis government, as defined in section 2 of the Recognition of Certain MÃ©tis Governments in Alberta, Ontario and Saskatchewan and MÃ©tis Self-Government Act, that is a party to a treaty, as defined in that section.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 23(2) of this Act comes into force before section 23 of the other Act, then that section 23 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 8(7) of the Privacy Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (j), by adding âorââ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the Yale First Nation Government, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Yale First Nation Final Agreement Act.

Subsection.Text: If section 23 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 23(2) of this Act, then that section 23 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 23(2) and subsection (5) applies as a consequence.

Section.Text: If Bill S-13, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Interpretation Act and to make related amendments to other Acts, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 1 of that Act and section 3.1 of this Act are in force, that section 3.1 is repealed.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: MÃ©tis Governments, MÃ©tis Collectivities, Treaties and Coming-into-force Dates

row.entry: MÃ©tis Communities Represented by the MÃ©tis Nation of Ontario
